key_insight: Neural network depth is adapted by treating training as an optimal control problem and inserting layers where a posteriori error estimates are largest.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled way to build surrogate models that target difficult regions of a scientific mapping, improving accuracy and generalization with fewer arbitrary architecture choices.
method: Formulate network training as a continuous-time optimal control problem with weights and biases varying piecewise across layers.
Derive dual-weighted-residual a posteriori error bounds that decompose total approximation error into interval-wise layer contributions.
Adapt depth by inserting new layers at locations of maximum estimated error.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on scientific datasets, including learning the observable-to-parameter map for Navier-Stokes, and the abstract reports consistently better generalization than existing architecture adaptation methods.
watch_item: The approach requires reliable error estimation and careful numerical implementation of the continuous-time/discrete-layer formulation, which may increase complexity.
paper_title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
